figures.caption: Fig. 1: Comparison of different method designs and their object removal results. Green represents the user-provided mask that covers the object to be removed. Details within the red and blue bounding boxes are shown in the rightmost column. Compared with DiffuEraser [22] and ROSE [27] that leave the human in the mirror and its shadow unremoved, our model successfully removes the human along with its induced effects.
Fig. 5: Samples from our real-world benchmark for video object removal.
Table 1: Quantitative comparisons on ROSE-Bench and our proposed CAMERABench and Scene-Bench.
Fig. 12: Effect of our Keyframe-Guided Propagation strategy on long videos.
Fig. 9: Effect of our OIRD (left) and FCCA (right).

Fig. 2: Towards side-effect-aware video object removal, we propose an understandingcentric video diffusion framework. Object-Induced Relation Distillation transfers object-side-effect relational knowledge from a vision foundation model to the diffusion denoiser, while Framewise Context Cross-Attention injects frame-specific unmasked background context to stabilize inpainting and improve spatiotemporal consistency.
